text:
There was a small town called Quiet Valley. Everybody knew each other. Granny Miller spun yarns all evening. One evening, she recounted a story about a wise old owl.

There once was an owl living high in the oldest oak tree. One day, a wise old woman rabbit wandered into the oak. She wanted to consult the owl about her yearly visit.

"Oh dear," the owl sighed. "I know everything but secrets. And secrets are hard things."

"Oh please talk!" she chirped.

"If you tell them everthing else about yourself, they won’t believe you. And believe me, nobody believes old owls."

She thought carefully. wasn't everything else terrible?

"Well," she said cautiously.

"The winter's chill was so great I couldn't fly.

I froze so deep you couldn’t hear me.

But I liked a little girl with purple feet.

That's why I talk sometimes."




That night, the owl fluttered a little closer. The girl sat quietly listening. Soon
